vocabulary.example_sentence: The wheel is an invention that changed how people travel.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 轮子是一项改变了人们出行方式的发明。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The first portable camera let families keep memories in photos.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 第一台便携相机让家庭能用照片留住回忆。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Her laptop stores hundreds of design files for the workshop.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 她的笔记本电脑存着作坊里数百个设计文件。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The high-speed train from Lhasa crosses the Tanggula Pass in hours.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 从拉萨出发的高铁几小时就穿越唐古拉山口。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The new printer is super fast at producing colour pages.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 新打印机出彩色页面超级快。

vocabulary.example_sentence: He wore sunglasses while testing the glider on a bright morning.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 晴朗的早晨试滑翔机时他戴了墨镜。

vocabulary.example_sentence: A mini-button on the phone opens the camera in one tap.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 手机上的迷你按钮一点就能打开相机。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Paper itself became cheaper after new production methods spread.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 造纸新方法普及后，纸本身变得更便宜。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Ancient papermaking turned plant fibre into sheets people could write on.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 古代造纸把植物纤维变成可书写的纸页。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Press the button and the machine prints your file.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 按下按钮，机器就会打印你的文件。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Hundreds of workshops in Europe once copied books by hand.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 欧洲曾有数百间作坊手工抄书。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Factories produce plastic parts for cameras and laptops.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 工厂生产相机和笔记本电脑的塑料零件。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Save the invention report as a file before you print it.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 打印前把发明报告存成文件。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Cheap plastic buttons made simple cameras affordable.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 便宜的塑料按钮让简易相机变得买得起。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The school printer ran out of paper during our history project.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 做历史课题时，学校打印机用完了纸。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Marconi helped invent radio communication across the sea.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 马可尼参与发明了跨洋无线电通信。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Paper spread all over the world after traders carried the skill abroad.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 商人把技艺带到海外后，纸张传遍了全世界。

vocabulary.example_sentence: I suppose the sticky note was created by accident in a lab.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 我想便利贴是在实验室里偶然造出来的。

vocabulary.english: be supposed to do / be sth
vocabulary.example_sentence: Ink is supposed to stay on paper, not on your shirt.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 墨水应该留在纸上，而不是沾在衬衫上。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Early carts with wheels moved stone blocks to build tunnels.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 带轮子的早期车辆把石块运到隧道工地。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Frozen ground made tunnel work slow until pipes carried warm air.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 冻土让隧道施工变慢，直到管道送来暖气。

vocabulary.chinese: 完成；结束；完全的；彻底的
vocabulary.example_sentence: It took years to complete the railway through the mountains.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 穿山铁路修完花了很多年。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Each layer of fibre makes handmade paper strong and thin.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 每层纤维让手工纸又薄又结实。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Heat can melt plastic shapes back into liquid for moulding.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 加热能把塑料形状再熔成液体以便塑形。

vocabulary.example_sentence: A pipe carried water to the papermaking workshop downstream.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 管道把水引到下游的造纸作坊。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Engineers dug a tunnel so trains could cross the high pass.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 工程师挖了隧道，让列车能穿越高山隘口。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Tourism grew rapidly once the high-speed line reached Lhasa.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 高铁通到拉萨后，旅游业迅速增长。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Inventors gain fame when their creations change daily life.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 发明改变日常生活时，发明者会赢得名声。

vocabulary.example_sentence: What seemed like fantasy became reality when phones could send photos.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 手机能发照片后，曾经的幻想变成了现实。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Papermaking workshops shaped the birth of printing in CE times.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 造纸作坊促成了公元时代印刷术的诞生。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Cai Lun is responsible for major improvements in early paper.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 蔡伦对早期纸张的重大改进负有重要责任。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Cheap fibre was responsible for paper prices falling sharply.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 廉价纤维导致纸价大幅下跌。

vocabulary.example_sentence: There was rapid growth in camera sales after colour film appeared.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 彩色胶片出现后，相机销量迅速增长。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Molten metal can shape bronze ware into bowls and bells.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 熔化的金属能把青铜铸成碗和钟。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Bronze bells from ancient workshops still ring in museums.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 古代作坊的青铜钟在博物馆里仍能敲响。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Delicate stone ware travelled along trade routes to Arabia.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 精致的石器沿商路传到阿拉伯地区。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Stone wheels ground fibre before it entered the paper vat.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 石磨在纤维入槽前把它磨碎。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Jute fibre from plant stems was once too coarse for fine paper.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 茎上的黄麻纤维一度太粗，做不出好纸。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Plant fibre soaked in liquid pulp became smooth sheets.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 植物纤维在液体浆里泡软后变成平滑纸页。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Before paper, books were expensive because scribes wrote each copy.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 纸出现前，书很贵，因为抄写员要逐本手抄。

vocabulary.example_sentence: A government officer recorded new inventions in the capital.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 朝廷官员在京师记录新发明。

vocabulary.example_sentence: In the workshop, workers layered pulp and pressed it dry.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 作坊里工人铺浆层并压干。

vocabulary.example_sentence: None of the early gliders flew as far as the Wright brothers' plane.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 早期滑翔机没有一架飞得像莱特兄弟的飞机那样远。

vocabulary.chinese: 最合适的；理想的；理想；完美的人（或事物）
vocabulary.example_sentence: Bamboo fibre was ideal for light, strong writing paper.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 竹纤维适合做轻而韧的书写纸。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Certain Arabic scholars copied papermaking steps into their notes.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 一些阿拉伯学者把造纸步骤抄进笔记。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Find a suitable liquid to dissolve the sticky glue on labels.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 找合适的液体溶解标签上的黏胶。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The pulp stayed liquid until workers lifted the screen.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 纸浆保持液态，直到工人提起筛网。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Cheap paper let schools print textbooks for every child.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 便宜的纸让学校能给每个孩子印课本。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The Post-it note is a creation born from a failed strong glue.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 便利贴这种创造来自一次失败强力胶的实验。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Later on, Bell's telephone reached homes across Oakland.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 后来，贝尔的电话进入了奥克兰千家万户。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Printing presses speed up the spread of ideas in Europe.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 印刷机加快了思想在欧洲的传播。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Arabic traders helped move paper technology westward.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 阿拉伯商人帮助把造纸技术向西传播。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Better glue did not result in a strong bond—it result in sticky notes.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 更好的胶没产生强黏合，却诞生了便利贴。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Cheaper books lead to more reading and wider education.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 更便宜的书带来更多阅读与更广的教育。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The birth of radio linked ships far from any shore.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 无线电的诞生让远离海岸的船只也能联络。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Mobile phones spread rapidly once cameras were built in.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 手机内置相机后迅速普及。

vocabulary.example_sentence: More families could afford a printer when prices dropped.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 价格下降后，更多家庭买得起打印机。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The company in Oakland turned weak glue into a bestseller.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 奥克兰那家公司把弱胶变成了畅销品。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Spencer Silver made a sticky adhesive that peeled off cleanly.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 斯潘塞·西尔弗做出一种能干净揭下的黏性胶。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Mass production lowered the cost of plastic sunglasses.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 大规模生产降低了塑料墨镜的成本。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Trains gradually replaced horse carts on long trade routes.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 在长途商路上，火车逐渐取代马车。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Paper is now used widely in offices, schools, and homes.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 纸如今在办公室、学校和家庭里广泛使用。

vocabulary.chinese: 试图找到做某事的方法
vocabulary.example_sentence: Inventors are looking to build quieter high-speed trains.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 发明者正设法打造更安静的高铁。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The Wright brothers tested control ideas on a glider before adding an engine.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 莱特兄弟在装发动机前用滑翔机试验操控想法。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Guglielmo Marconi sent the first wireless signal across the Atlantic.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 古列尔莫·马可尼发送了首条跨大西洋无线电信号。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Alexander Bell patented the telephone in the nineteenth century.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 亚历山大·贝尔在十九世纪为电话申请了专利。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Paper mills spread across Europe after the skill left the East.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 技艺东传西走后，造纸厂遍布欧洲。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Travellers take the high-speed train to Lhasa for its mountain views.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 旅客乘高铁去拉萨欣赏山景。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The Tanggula Pass is one of the highest points on the railway.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 唐古拉山口是铁路沿线最高点之一。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Papermaking skills were documented in China long before 1000 CE.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 造纸技艺在中国公元一千年之前就有记载。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Arthur Fry first used the sticky note in his office in Oakland.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 亚瑟·弗赖伊在奥克兰的办公室里首次使用了便利贴。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Spencer Silver invented the low-tack glue behind Post-it notes.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 斯潘塞·西尔弗发明了便利贴背后的低黏胶。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Arthur Fry used the weak glue to mark pages in his choir book.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 亚瑟·弗赖伊用弱胶在合唱谱里标记页码。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Orville Wright piloted the first powered flight at Kitty Hawk.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 奥维尔·莱特在基蒂霍克驾驶了首次动力飞行。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Wilbur Wright solved wing design problems before the famous flight.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 威尔伯·莱特在著名飞行前解决了机翼设计问题。
